note: Daily MarCRM scrape — new lead discovery + signal refresh on hot existing leads. Quality over quantity: 5 solid leads across pr-freelance (1), pr-marketing (3), legal-freelance (1). Top of the funnel today is the Lemonade Senior Communications Manager 6-month contract (combined 90) — explicit fractional/contract intent at a public fintech-adjacent brand. New pr-marketing standout is Bunch (private-markets fund-ops platform) closing its $35M Series B from Portage on the day of the scrape. Plus a fresh Latitude Legal Securities Counsel (SEC + Blue Sky, part-time remote) posting that fits Mark's fractional engagement model. Signal refresh covers 5 hot leads from prior reports — most notably Fasset's confirmed headcount-tripling expansion plan and Lemonade's parallel hiring of both a full-time Sr Director PR AND a 6-month contract Sr Comms Manager (rare dual-track signal). Mom Project Transactional Attorney intentionally skipped: channel mismatch (Mom Project is a returner network for parents — Mark's profile not a fit for that channel, though the underlying client may still be reachable).

leads.name: Lemonade — Senior Communications Manager (6-Month Contract, NYC)
leads.industry: Insurance technology — AI-driven P&C insurance (renters, homeowners, pet, car, life); publicly traded NYSE: LMND
leads.fundingStage: Public company (NYSE: LMND); active comms function scaling — parallel hiring of full-time Senior Director of Communications & PR AND this 6-month contract Senior Communications Manager
leads.compensationText: not stated in posting; comparable senior comms contracts in NYC market $8k–$15k/month or $150–$250/hr equivalent
leads.source: Lemonade Makers careers portal — Senior Communications Manager listing
leads.notes: Mark's bullseye for today. The 6-month contract framing is unusually clean intent — Lemonade is parallel-hiring a permanent Senior Director PR AND this 6-month contract role, meaning they have a defined interim coverage need they're willing to outsource. Sector fit is strong (insurance tech is finance-adjacent, B2C with strong brand DNA, post-IPO comms maturity). The win condition is positioning Mark's bench as a fractional/interim alternative — could be packaged as 90-day comms sprint + extension. Cross-reference: prior pr-freelance entry for 'Lemonade' on 2026-05-18 was scored 90 against the Senior Director PR full-time role; this new contract posting confirms (rather than duplicates) the comms scaling thesis. Recommend opening a single Lemonade account in the CRM and threading both roles under it.

leads.name: Bunch
leads.industry: Fintech — AI-native fund operations platform for private markets (PE/VC fund administration, capital calls, compliance, distributions, reporting)
leads.location: Berlin, Germany (HQ); recent UK launch; expanding to Luxembourg
leads.fundingStage: Series B — $35M (€30.1M) closed 2026-05-19, led by Portage with Illuminate Financial and follow-on from Motive Partners, Cherry Ventures, FinTech Collective, angels. Total funding now $58M+
leads.source: fintech.global / IBS Intelligence / BeBeez International — Bunch Series B coverage
leads.notes: Top new pr-marketing lead today. Bunch hit a near-perfect FIT profile: AI-native private-markets infrastructure (the exact intersection of Mark's fintech ICP), Series B+UK launch double trigger announced same day, proof points are clean (300% ARR, 156% NRR, 12K LPs already on platform). INTENT is held at 30 because Mark hasn't engaged yet and the company is Berlin-HQ (timezone friction, may have local German PR firm). Wedge: Bunch is making explicit anti-BPO arguments via their own blog ('The BPO model is breaking: How AI is rewriting fund administration') — they already think category-creation, which is exactly the playbook Mark sells. Push for a Tuesday/Wednesday outbound this week; the press window is wide-open for ~2 weeks. Likely already have a European PR firm — Mark's wedge is US/North America (LP audience, US LPs, US press cycle for AI-native fintech infra).

leads.name: Latitude Legal — Securities Counsel, SEC & Blue Sky Compliance (Part-Time, Remote) #6998
leads.industry: Alternative legal services provider (ALSP) — engagement role supporting a global law firm client on securities compliance work
leads.fundingStage: Active part-time contract engagement posted 2026-05 via Latitude Legal direct careers; supporting securities compliance for a global law firm client (private placements, Form D, Blue Sky)
leads.compensationText: $225,000–$275,000 annualized on a full-time basis; pro-rated for 20+ hr/week. Latitude contractors are W2 with benefits when working 20+ hrs/week.
leads.notes: Clean part-time remote securities-counsel posting via Latitude (Mark's most reliable legal-freelance source). FIT is moderated by the fact that this is securities-compliance (not fund formation) and the buyer is an ALSP placing into a law firm, not a direct fund/operating-company client — but INTENT is near-max: explicit part-time, remote, comp stated, direct apply path Mark already knows. Mark may already be in Latitude's pool; if so, the action is to flag this specific engagement to his Latitude relationship rather than apply cold. CAVEAT: 10+ years securities experience required — confirm Mark's depth in SEC private-placement and Blue Sky work before applying.

leads.name: Searchable
leads.industry: AI-native analytics & optimization for AI-search platforms (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity) — answer engine optimization / generative engine optimization category
leads.fundingStage: Series A — $14M closed 2026-05-18 led by Headline, valuing the company at $85M
leads.source: Tech Startups — Top startup and tech funding news, May 18 2026
leads.notes: AEO/GEO (answer engine / generative engine optimization) is one of the hottest emerging marketing categories of 2026 — the entire enterprise SEO budget is in motion. Searchable's $14M Series A at $85M post is a clean trigger and the category is being defined this quarter. FIT score moderated because the company is early ($14M Series A — budget will be conservative until they hit Series B revenue threshold) and proof points are still emerging. INTENT bumped on urgency because the press window for AEO/GEO category leadership closes fast — first-mover narrative is being claimed right now (Profound, Otterly, Athena Intelligence all in the same space). Limited public info available — no website, no contact yet — but Headline VC is a known Mark-network connection point. Recommend a 30-min discovery call rather than a full pitch — confirm budget readiness before committing to a 90-day plan.

leads.name: LemFi
leads.industry: Fintech — cross-border payments / remittance platform for immigrants and global citizens (US, UK, Canada, 30+ corridor markets)
leads.location: Toronto, Canada (HQ); offices in US, UK
leads.fundingStage: Series B extension — €30M (~$32M USD) extension reportedly closing, on top of the $53M Series B closed January 2025 (Highland Europe led; Left Lane, Palm Drive, Endeavor Catalyst, YC participated). Total Series B+extension ~$85M.
leads.source: Tech Funding News / FintechFutures — LemFi Series B extension scoop
leads.notes: Solid B-grade pr-marketing lead. Cross-border / immigrant fintech is a sympathetic press storyline with a clear founder narrative (Olalere + Cochran, ex-OPay). FIT is solid (B2C+B2B, 2M+ users, $1B monthly volume = serious proof). INTENT moderated because (a) this is an EXTENSION rather than fresh round, dampens the announcement-cycle press window; (b) raise is reported as 'reportedly closing' rather than confirmed closed — press window opens fully on official close. Wedge: position Mark as the US/UK press partner for the next 12 months of expansion + credit-product launch (the Pillar-enabled credit storyline is a strong narrative no one has owned yet). Likely already has a UK PR firm via Highland Europe network — Mark's North American narrative wedge is the differentiator. Recommend waiting until official close announcement (likely next 1-2 weeks) before formal pitch.
